game_name: Super Star Force: Jikūreki no Himitsu

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile airborne entities unique to specific time periods
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or destruction during aerial segments

danger_signals.visual_form: Savage demons, behemoths, and supernatural entities in labyrinth environments
danger_signals.meaning: Ground-based combat threats in dungeon exploration segments

danger_signals.visual_form: Volcanic craters, underwater eruptions, Earth crust deformations
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental dangers tied to world-state progression; hazards that can cascade through time

danger_signals.visual_form: Physical barriers (e.g., gate blocking temple entrance)
danger_signals.meaning: Progression blocked; requires time-consumption or item acquisition to bypass

danger_signals.visual_form: Evil planet entity; appears as final boss in AD 2137
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonistic force; ultimate threat to timeline stability

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC in red suit of armor with horned helmet
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven for item purchase, hints, and progression guidance

safety_signals.visual_form: Underground labyrinths, temples, enclosed exploration spaces
safety_signals.meaning: Transition from aerial combat to controlled dungeon exploration; shift in threat model

safety_signals.visual_form: Fetal Movement era; volcanic empire region with no time point consumption
safety_signals.meaning: Safe temporal anchor point; can return without resource cost

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden items obtained from level dungeons
safety_signals.meaning: Progress markers; enable advancement and final boss confrontation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Final boss form; represents temporal corruption
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Manifestations across multiple time periods; can resurrect/persist
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in AD 2137 as primary antagonist; affects timeline stability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Legendary civilization; volcanic crater entrance marks territory
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: DA 0001; distinctive volcanoes; no infinite-loop level design suggests primordial nature

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears as civilizational threat; shaped by behemoth Ganimu
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: DA 1608 (Ice Age); destroyed civilization; player actions in DA 1301 affect their survival

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sunken civilization; represents lost advanced culture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: DA 1301; submerged oceanic environment; crust deformation causes submersion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Time-traveling spacecraft; Neo Final Star vessel
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Agency across all time periods; actions create branching timeline outcomes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Volcanic landscape with underground slave empire; distinctive crater architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No time-consumption required to return; acts as safe temporal anchor; non-looping flight segments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rugged mountains; inorganic ground materials; giant ground drawings depicting deity Birakocha worship
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Savage demons defend labyrinth; primitive civilization theme affects enemy design

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Monuments to past civilizations; Erudorera Forest Demon ruins with closed entrance; Statue of Dorera as key item
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unique airborne enemies exclusive to this era; critical progression bottleneck; requires pre-acquired items

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lush jungle environment; mystery temple (Jukai Madagu-Tem Oates) with obscured scenery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Temple entrance blocked by gate; gate opens by consuming time units; environmental puzzle integration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Oceanic environment; sunken Lemurian continent beneath surface; visible egg form of behemoth Ganimu
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player action (destroying Ganimu egg) creates branching timeline; prevents future civilization extinction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Frozen landscape; ruins of destroyed Boolean Shang city-state; environmental desolation markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Civilization already destroyed by adult Ganimu; represents consequence state if player fails DA 1301 intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gordess as visible final boss entity; culmination of all prior timeline manipulations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple endings available: happy (history saved) or sad (Gordess resurrection confirmed); outcome determined by prior actions

environmental_storytelling.example: Ganimu egg in DA 1301 (harmless) vs. adult Ganimu-destroyed civilization in DA 1608; shows player causality across time

Progression from primitive Erujinba (DA 0001) → Genesis demons (DA 0316) → monument-filled ruins (DA 0820) → advanced Lemurian settlement (DA 1301) → post-apocalyptic ice age (DA 1608); visual archaeology of timeline
Closed temple gates, blocked dungeon entrances, sealed ruins; physical environment enforces progression logic and time-resource economy
Giant ground drawings of Birakocha in DA 0316; indicates cultural beliefs shape civilization visual identity
Lemurian continent visible in ocean (DA 1301) → implied to be submerged by era transition; Earth deformation shown through geography shifts

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Pilot controlling Neo Final Star; shoots enemies; traverses between time periods; score = temporal currency
Foot-based exploration; item collection; puzzle-solving; gate interaction; shift to adventure game mechanics
Score/time units halved; penalty for aerial combat failure; continue available without resource loss (unless console reset)
Sells items; provides hints; wears red armor with horned helmet; represents stable information/commerce anchor
Occupy enclosed dungeon spaces; block progression; tied to primitive civilization archetype
Destroyable; non-hostile; player action creates timeline branch preventing future extinction
Destroys Boolean Shang civilization; appears as region-destroying force; manifestation of past-action consequence
Resurrection threat; can persist through ages; faces player in AD 2137; outcome determines happy vs. sad ending
Gated progression items; required to advance to final confrontation; represent puzzle completion markers

notes: Super Star Force uses temporal world-state as primary storytelling mechanic. Visual manifestation is tied to era design (geological features, civilization architecture, environmental decay). Danger signals are distributed across time periods; safety is anchored to specific zones (DA 0001, Norm's presence). The game's hybrid shmup-adventure structure creates two behavioral manifestation modes (aerial combat vs. grounded exploration), each with distinct visual communication systems. Factions are primarily civilizational/temporal rather than political entities proper—Gordess represents a corruption threat spanning all timelines, while civilizations (Lemurian, Boolean Shang, Erujinba) serve as environment backdrops and consequence indicators. Player agency directly shapes visual outcomes (egg destruction prevents future civilization collapse), making world-state responsive to player action rather than fixed narrative.
